name: Cut cloud bill by 50% without breaking the app
description: Startup's AWS bill has climbed to $8K/month. Breakdown: $3K EC2 (mostly idle m5.xlarge instances), $2K RDS (over-provisioned Postgres), $1.5K CloudFront egress, $1K S3, $0.5K misc. The team is 5 engineers, so engineering time is expensive. Need to cut bill to $4K/month.
task: Audit the $8K/month cloud spend and propose concrete changes to reach $4K/month without major downtime. For each recommendation provide: (1) current cost, (2) proposed change, (3) expected savings, (4) implementation steps, (5) risk level. Include the exact CLI commands or Terraform changes. Prioritize by savings-to-effort ratio. The team values engineer time over cloud savings at <$200/month.
expected_outcomes: Identifies biggest cost drivers with savings estimates
Proposes concrete instance right-sizing or reserved capacity
Calls out CloudFront egress or caching fixes
